message:
chore(search-callers): wire include_highlights through unified query + web search handler

Threads the new `SearchOptions::include_highlights` field (introduced in
6a0590d2 "perf(frankenterm-core/storage): two-stage FTS search …") to
the two existing callers that build SearchOptions.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/query_contract.rs
  `to_storage_search_options(&UnifiedSearchQuery)` now sets
  `include_highlights: Some(query.snippets)`. This preserves the prior
  behavior — when the unified query has snippets disabled, the two-stage
  path skips `highlight()` entirely; when snippets are on, callers still
  receive the highlighted column the same way they did before.
  Added a short comment pointing at ft-okhhj for the two-stage rationale.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/handlers.rs
  `handle_search` opts the web UI out of `highlight()` materialization
  by setting `include_highlights: Some(false)`. The web search already
  renders only the snippet column, so the full highlighted segment was
  pure waste on every query. Snippets remain on
  (`include_snippets: Some(true)`).

No production behavior change for query_contract callers; web search
now skips one FTS5 function call per row.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -306,6 +306,10 @@ pub fn to_storage_search_options(query: &UnifiedSearchQuery) -> SearchOptions {
         since: query.since,
         until: query.until,
         include_snippets: Some(query.snippets),
+        // Highlights default to mirroring snippets (preserves prior
+        // unified-query behavior); two-stage path skips highlight()
+        // entirely when `query.snippets == false` (ft-okhhj).
+        include_highlights: Some(query.snippets),
         snippet_max_tokens: Some(SEARCH_SNIPPET_MAX_TOKENS),
         highlight_prefix: Some(SEARCH_HIGHLIGHT_PREFIX.to_string()),
         highlight_suffix: Some(SEARCH_HIGHLIGHT_SUFFIX.to_string()),

@@ -293,6 +293,7 @@ pub(super) fn handle_search(
         since: parse_i64(&qs, "since"),
         until: parse_i64(&qs, "until"),
         include_snippets: Some(true),
+        include_highlights: Some(false),
         snippet_max_tokens: Some(64),
         highlight_prefix: None,
         highlight_suffix: None,
